title: Thelypteris cataractorum
intro: Thelypteris cataractorum, also known as Cataract Fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris cataractorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris cataractorum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures and extracts, to treat fever, pain, and other inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Thelypteris cataractorum is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of ferns commonly known as marsh ferns. The species is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. Thelypteris cataractorum is a relatively small fern that typically grows in wet environments, such as near rivers or in marshy areas. The classification of Thelypteris cataractorum is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphology.
